repository_ssim: St. Catherine's Monastery of the Sinai
titles_tesim: John Chrysostom, Homily on the Rich and the Poor
contents_tesim: Homily on the Rich and the Poor?
It may be the case that this homily follows a dormition narrative; compare Sinai Arabic 556, which has a homily by John Chrysostom on the rich and the poor that follows a dormition narrative
John Chrysostom, Homily on the Rich and the Poor
The undertext appears to contain a homily on the rich and the poor and seems to refer to the narrative of the rich man and Lazarus in Luke 16:19-31
full_text_tesim: Combination of early Naskh and Maghribi scripts | Same script as UTO Homily on the Dormition. Large bowls with sweeping curves for a number of descending strokes. Occasionally the stems of the letter alif have a club-like extension to the left of their top point. Many times the loop of letter Ḍād and ṣād has no “tooth”. The base stroke of the initial letter ‘ayn is elongated backward to the right. Sporadic diacritical marks
